decisions.decision: Digital-resource write authz = Option B (role-based JWT gate), not Option A (org-based)
decisions.rationale: No person->org linkage exists in the schema; Option A is a data-model build, not a code change. Option B closes the IDOR minimally and matches the codebase's role-based guard convention. Option A deferred to SEED-012.

decisions.decision: Completed the paused resolver NaiveDateTime->DateTime<Utc> migration; backed the prematurely-started 11-04 CORS change out to HEAD
decisions.rationale: DB cols are TIMESTAMPTZ (DateTime<Utc>); the CORS change used the wrong rocket_cors API and belongs to 11-04. PolicyVersion kept NaiveDateTime for TS golden byte-parity.

context_notes: Resumed from a prior pause that stopped at 11-03 task 3/3 with a confirmed IDOR + an undecided authz model. This session: investigated the live DB (proved no person->org linkage), chose Option B with the user (Hybrid: fix now + SEED-012 for Option A), applied the role-based gate, completed the half-done resolver DateTime migration, simplified build_resolver_resource to an existence probe, rewrote the integration tests for role-based authz, backed out the 11-04 CORS start, de-hardcoded seedWorld, wrote 11-03-SUMMARY, updated STATE/ROADMAP. Verified: resolver_parity 1 passed (byte-exact), security tests pass on live DB (non-admin->403 grants+delegates, admin->404 clears gate), world-state vitest 8 passed, backend lib compiles clean. Then started the full stack for the user. Pre-existing/out-of-scope issues left untouched: organizations/handlers.rs inline unit tests miss a `department` field (breaks lib UNIT-test target only, not the integration test binaries); person/handlers.rs:257 unused-params warning; 6 react-refresh lint errors in world-state.tsx; wide backend/*.rs git drift predates this session. Phase 11 is 3/4 plans done; only 11-04 remains.
